description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a single build stage and a MOVIE_URL variable. Add a centos7 job that runs on the centos:7 image, only on the master or devel branches, installs the RPM Fusion repo and curl, make, gcc‑c++, ffmpeg‑devel, gd‑devel, zlib‑devel and libpng‑devel via yum, then changes to src and runs `make ENABLE_WEBP=0 ENABLE_AVIF=0`; it stores the ./bin/* files as an artifact named mtn-$CI_JOB_NAME that expires in four weeks. Add a debian job that runs on the debian:12 image, only when $CI_PROJECT_NAMESPACE equals "movie_thumbnailer", executes a before_script that records build info, updates apt, installs a long list of development packages and downloads a sample video, then clones specific FFmpeg and GD branches, patches GD, builds FFmpeg and GD with make and cmake, builds the static test binary, cleans up, creates mtn-static.tar.gz and moves any jpg files, and publishes ./bin/mtn, ./mtn-static.tar.gz and all .jpg files as a four‑week artifact, with a manual trigger. Add an fc_latest job on the fedora image that installs the RPM Fusion repo and the same build dependencies via dnf, runs `cd src && make`, and uploads ./bin/* as a four‑week artifact, also limited to master and devel. Add u18.04 and ubuntu_devel jobs on ubuntu:18.04 and ubuntu:devel images respectively, both only on master or devel, install curl, gcc, make and various libgd and ffmpeg development libraries via apt, then run `cd src && make $make_opt` (where make_opt is set to ENABLE_WEBP=0 ENABLE_AVIF=0 for u18.04), produce the same artifact and are manual. Finally, add a win job on the fedora image that runs only when $CI_PROJECT_NAMESPACE equals "movie_thumbnailer", installs curl, make and mingw64‑gcc, downloads prebuilt Windows GD and FFmpeg libraries, extracts them, builds with `make -f Makefile.mingw`, packages the results into a mtn-win64 directory, and publishes that directory as a four‑week artifact, also manual.
